data.title: AstraZeneca's Drug Pipeline in Focus After Recent Trial Miss — Earnings Preview
Safety Insurance Group to Be Acquired by MAPFRE USA for $105 Per Share in All-Cash Deal
data.description:
Safety Insurance Group entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger with MAPFRE U.S.A. Under the deal, Splash Merger Sub will merge into Safety Insurance Group, and shareholders will receive $105.00 in cash per share, with the company becoming a wholly owned subsidiary of MAPFRE U.S.A. The transaction is subject to shareholder approval, antitrust clearance, approval by the Massachusetts Commissioner of Insurance, and other customary conditions.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Agreement and Plan of Merger
  • Counterparty: MAPFRE U.S.A.
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 23 2026 / N/A
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing
  • Reason: Take-private sale to strategic buyer for cash

Original SEC Filing: SAFETY INSURANCE GROUP INC [ SAFT ] - 8-K - Jul. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Sadot Group Settles Rocket Claim for $500,000 in Stock, Resolves CFO Note With Shares and New Note
data.description:
Sadot Group announced two debt resolution agreements to streamline its balance sheet. The company settled a disputed claim with Rocket Capital NY by issuing 26,581 shares against an agreed $500,000 amount, with the related New York lawsuit to be dismissed and mutual releases in place. Separately, Sadot resolved a matured note and default interest owed to former CFO Jennifer Black by issuing 26,199 shares to settle $466,617.73 and providing a $409,082.17 unsecured promissory note for unpaid severance at 10% interest, due in one year. Both Rocket and Black agreed to sell no more than 15% of daily Nasdaq trading volume in any single day.

Agreement 1: Sadot Group Settles Rocket Claim for $500,000 in Stock; Lawsuit to Be Dismissed

  • Agreement type: Debt Settlement and Share Issuance Agreement
  • Counterparty: Rocket Capital NY
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 22 2026 / Jul 22 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Resolve disputed obligation and end litigation

Agreement 2: Sadot Group Resolves $466,618 Note Default to Former CFO With Shares; Issues $409,082 Note

  • Agreement type: Debt Settlement and Share Issuance Agreement; Unsecured one-year promissory note
  • Counterparty: Jennifer Black
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 23 2026 / Jul 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will (settlement); Severance Note: 1 year
  • Reason: Settle matured note and severance liabilities

Original SEC Filing: Sadot Group Inc. [ SDOT ] - 8-K - Jul. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Republic Reports Q2 2026 Net Income $32.9M; Diluted EPS $1.68
data.description:
Republic reported second-quarter 2026 net income of $32.9 million and diluted earnings per Class A common share of $1.68, up 4% versus the year-ago quarter. Return on average assets was 1.88% and return on average equity was 11.42% for the quarter. Total assets were approximately $7.06 billion as of June 30, 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $32.9 million for Q2 2026, up 4% from $31.5 million in Q2 2025.
  • Diluted EPS: $1.68 for Q2 2026, up $0.07 (4%) from $1.61 in Q2 2025.
  • Income before income tax expense: $42.55 million for Q2 2026, up $2.16 million (5%) year-over-year.
  • Return on average assets (ROA): 1.88% for Q2 2026; Return on average equity (ROE): 11.42% for Q2 2026.
  • Allowance for credit losses on loans (Total Company): $87.8 million, representing 1.62% of total loans as of June 30, 2026.

Business Highlights

  • Core Bank net income: $22.8 million in Q2 2026, a 22% increase from Q2 2025, driven by net interest income growth and lower provision for credit losses.
  • Core Bank net interest income: $64.1 million in Q2 2026, up $4.2 million (7%) year-over-year; Core Bank net interest margin expanded to 4.02% (up 30 basis points).
  • Traditional Banking loans grew $59 million during the quarter (average loans $4.647 billion; period-end $4.656 billion), the strongest quarterly loan growth in nearly three years.
  • Launched Republic Payment Solutions (RPS) in June 2026; expected to contribute over $150 million of lower-cost average deposits during the remainder of 2026 and improve funding diversification.
  • Republic Processing Group results: RPG net income $10.1 million (down from $12.8 million a year earlier) with segment variances — TRS seasonality and lower RPS yields impacted results while RCS grew originations but saw higher provisions.

Original SEC Filing: REPUBLIC BANCORP INC /KY/ [ RBCAA ] - 8-K - Jul. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Noodles & Company Q2 2026: Revenue $127.0M, System-wide comps +10.3%, Adjusted EBITDA $10.8M
data.description:
Noodles & Company reported second-quarter 2026 results with total revenue of $127.0 million and system-wide comparable restaurant sales up 10.3%. The company posted a net loss of $4.0 million (loss per diluted share $0.67) while adjusted EBITDA rose to $10.8 million. Management raised full-year 2026 guidance and highlighted continued comparable-sales momentum and margin improvement.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $127.0 million for Q2 2026, up 0.5% from $126.4 million in Q2 2025.
  • Net loss: $4.0 million for Q2 2026, or $0.67 loss per diluted share (Q2 2025 net loss $17.6 million, $3.04 loss per diluted share).
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $10.8 million for Q2 2026, a 79% increase from $6.0 million in Q2 2025.
  • Operating margin: (1.2)% in Q2 2026 versus (11.7)% in Q2 2025.
  • Restaurant contribution margin: 17.2% in Q2 2026 versus 12.8% in Q2 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Comparable restaurant sales: System-wide comps increased 10.3%, driven by an 11.4% increase at company-owned restaurants and a 5.5% increase at franchise restaurants.
  • Traffic and momentum: The company reported seven consecutive fiscal quarters of positive comparable-restaurant-sales growth and continued strong traffic; quarter-to-date company-owned comps in Q3 are up ~10%.
  • Portfolio optimization: Company plans to close 30–35 company-owned restaurants and five franchised restaurants in 2026 as part of portfolio optimization.
  • Unit counts: 318 company-owned restaurants and 78 franchise restaurants as of June 30, 2026, reflecting ongoing refranchising and optimization activity.
  • Liquidity and capital actions: As of June 30, 2026, cash and cash equivalents were $1.3 million, outstanding debt was $105.4 million, and $16.6 million was available under the revolving credit facility; management expects to use free cash flow to reduce debt toward a target at or below three times 2026 adjusted EBITDA.

Original SEC Filing: NOODLES & Co [ NDLS ] - 8-K - Jul. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Lamb Weston posts FY2026 revenue $6.61B, adjusted EBITDA $1.15B and adjusted EPS $3.01
data.description:
Lamb Weston reported fiscal 2026 net sales of $6,612.3 million and adjusted EBITDA of $1,147.2 million, with adjusted diluted EPS of $3.01. GAAP net income for the year was $290.0 million and diluted EPS was $2.08. The company said net sales and adjusted EBITDA exceeded the high end of its guidance and delivered volume growth in North America despite international headwinds.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales (FY2026): $6,612.3 million.
  • Gross profit (FY2026): $1,359.7 million (as reported).
  • Income from operations (FY2026): $591.1 million (as reported).
  • Net income (FY2026, GAAP): $290.0 million; diluted EPS: $2.08.
  • Adjusted results (FY2026): Adjusted EBITDA $1,147.2 million; Adjusted diluted EPS $3.01; Adjusted net income $419.8 million.

Business Highlights

  • North America volume growth: Sales volume increased for the sixth consecutive quarter and drove full-year and Q4 top-line growth, with North America net sales up 9% in Q4 and 3% for the full year.
  • International challenges: EMEA markets were negatively impacted by Middle East conflict and competitive conditions, leading to lower international sales and a decline in international adjusted EBITDA.
  • Operational improvements: Supply chain and manufacturing productivity gains reduced manufacturing cost per pound and supported increased North America segment adjusted EBITDA margin.
  • Cost Savings Program: Launched Focus to Win in July 2025 with a three-year cost savings target of at least $250 million annualized by end of fiscal 2028; exceeded the $100 million fiscal 2026 milestone.
  • Capital and shareholder actions: Capital expenditures of $410.1 million in fiscal 2026 (down from prior year); returned $320.7 million to shareholders in fiscal 2026 via $207.5 million dividends and $113.2 million repurchases; $245 million remains authorized for repurchase.

Original SEC Filing: Lamb Weston Holdings, Inc. [ LW ] - 8-K - Jul. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Gentex reports Q2 2026: Revenue $651.3M, Gross margin 37.0%, Adj EPS $0.58
data.description:
Gentex reported second-quarter 2026 results with net sales of $651.3 million and GAAP net income attributable to Gentex of $114.7 million. The company recorded a 37.0% gross margin and adjusted diluted earnings per share of $0.58 for the quarter. Management highlighted share repurchases of $66.0 million and continued growth in non-automotive product lines.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $651.3 million for the three months ended June 30, 2026 (vs. $657.9 million in Q2 2025).
  • Gross profit: $241.0 million; Gross margin: 37.0% (up 280 basis points vs. Q2 2025).
  • Income from operations (GAAP): $141.3 million; Adjusted income from operations (non-GAAP): $141.7 million.
  • Net income attributable to Gentex (GAAP): $114.7 million; Adjusted net income (non-GAAP): $122.9 million.
  • Adjusted diluted earnings per share (non-GAAP): $0.58; GAAP diluted EPS: $0.54.

Business Highlights

  • Automotive revenue declined ~3% quarter over quarter, driven by weaker performance in Europe, Japan/Korea and China, partially offset by strength in North America.
  • Premium Audio delivered meaningful growth: Premium Audio revenue rose about 16% quarter over quarter to $51.7 million, led by Powered Systems and Onkyo brands.
  • Other Products category (aerospace, biometrics, fire protection, medical devices, automotive aftermarket) grew ~12% quarter over quarter to $39.4 million, with aerospace and biometric/accessory gains offsetting lower aftermarket sales.
  • Auto-dimming mirror unit shipments: total units declined ~10% year-over-year for the quarter; international interior mirror shipments fell 26% quarter over quarter while North America increased.
  • Capital allocation and operations: repurchased 2.7 million shares for $66.0 million in Q2 (5.9 million shares YTD for $137.6 million) and updated full-year 2026 guidance narrowing gross margin and capex ranges while keeping revenue guidance at $2.65–$2.75 billion.

Original SEC Filing: GENTEX CORP [ GNTX ] - 8-K - Jul. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Dyadic Regains Nasdaq Compliance, Shares to Remain Listed
data.description:
Dyadic regained compliance with Nasdaq listing rules and will continue trading on The Nasdaq Capital Market.

Key Highlights:

  • Nasdaq confirmed Dyadic has regained compliance with Rules 5550(a)(2) and 5550(b).
  • Dyadic’s common stock will continue to be listed and traded on The Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • Management says restored listing stability allows focus on executing strategy and commercializing C1 and Dapibus platforms.
  • Company intends to advance strategic collaborations and product commercialization to create long-term shareholder value.

Original SEC Filing: DYADIC INTERNATIONAL INC [ DYAI ] - 8-K - Jul. 24, 2026

data.title: Blackstone Real Estate Income Trust posts strong June flows, 10.3% 1-year Class I return
data.description:
Blackstone Real Estate Income Trust reported accelerating returns with a 10.3% Class I net return over the last year and positive June performance.

Key Highlights:

  • Delivered 10.3% Class I net return over the trailing 12 months; June net return 1.1% and H1 2026 Class I return 5.2%.
  • Generated 4.6% annualized distribution rate (Class I); 2025 distributions were 100% return of capital, implying a 7.3% tax-equivalent rate.
  • Recorded five consecutive months of positive net flows and Q2 2026 was first quarter of positive net flows in nearly four years.
  • Portfolio ~90% concentrated in rental housing, industrial and data centers; deployed $5.7B into pre-leased data center developments in H1 2026.

Original SEC Filing: Blackstone Real Estate Income Trust, Inc. [ BSTT ] - 8-K - Jul. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: LW: Fiscal 2026 beat sales and EBITDA guidance, with North America gains offsetting EMEA challenges
data.description:
Revised summary:
Fiscal 2026 net sales and adjusted EBITDA exceeded guidance, driven by strong North America growth offsetting international challenges. Net income and adjusted EBITDA declined year-over-year due to inflation and EMEA headwinds, but cost savings supported margins. Fiscal 2027 guidance projects flat to modest growth, focusing on cost efficiency and shareholder returns.

data.title: BANKBARODA: Net profit fell on exceptional provisioning, but deposits, advances, and asset quality improved
data.description:
Net profit declined sharply due to a one-time exceptional provision, despite strong growth in deposits and advances. Asset quality improved, capital adequacy remained robust, and digital and sustainability initiatives advanced.

data.title: LBTYA: Strong Q2 execution, asset monetizations, and Ziggo spin-off progress amid mixed segment results
data.description:
Q2 2026 saw strong operational execution, with progress on the Ziggo Group spin-off, robust asset monetizations, and improved cash targets. Segment results were mixed, with Telenet and VodafoneZiggo showing commercial momentum, while VMO2 and Wyre faced revenue and EBITDA pressures amid competitive markets.

Original document: Liberty Global Ltd. [LBTYA] SEC 8-K Current Report — Jul. 24 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Lamb Weston Holdings Announces Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
The Board of Directors of Lamb Weston Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: LW) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.38 per share of Lamb Weston common stock. The dividend is payable on September 4, 2026, to stockholders of record as of the close of business on August 7, 2026.

About Lamb Weston
Lamb Weston is a leading supplier of frozen potato products to restaurants and retailers around the world. For more than 75 years, Lamb Weston has led the industry in innovation, introducing inventive products that simplify back-of-house management for its customers and make things more delicious for their customers. From the fields where Lamb Weston potatoes are grown to proactive customer partnerships, Lamb Weston always strives for more and never settles. Because, when we look at a potato, we see possibilities. Learn more about us at lambweston.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260714973913/en/

data.title: Lamb Weston Holdings Announces Quarterly Dividend
